description_long: This object is a vintage beige computer monitor, reminiscent of early Macintosh models, with a boxy, rectangular form and a slightly protruding front display. The front features a large, recessed glass screen framed by a thick, rounded plastic bezel, with a narrow slot and a small rectangular button or port beneath the screen. The rear panel is vented for cooling, with a prominent handle integrated into the top for carrying, and a vertical switch or lever on the right side. The sides are plain, with subtle angular lines and a slight taper toward the back. The overall surface is smooth, matte plastic, with minimal decorative elements and no visible branding.
description: A vintage beige computer monitor with a thick, rounded bezel, large glass screen, and vented rear panel with a carrying handle.
description_view_0: The left side of the monitor is plain, showing the boxy shape and subtle angular lines with no visible controls or features.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the large glass screen, thick rounded bezel, and a slot with a small button or port beneath the display.
description_view_2: The right side mirrors the left but includes a visible vertical switch or lever near the rear edge.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the vented panel, integrated carrying handle, and multiple ports or connectors along the bottom edge, as well as the vertical switch or lever.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rounded, and made of matte beige plastic, with a smooth surface and no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor has an integrated flat base, forming part of the main body, with no visible separate stand or adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A single button or port is located beneath the screen on the front lower right, and a vertical switch or lever is present on the rear right side.
attribute_port_visibility: Visible on the rear lower panel, with multiple ports or connectors present.
